AuthClass.note: Derived from the CREATED access-control / KeyInfo, never echoed from the request.

KeySpec.attestationChallenge: bytes? (bound at generation; there is no call-time challenge)

variants.strongest: Device's best hardware tier; fails unavailableTier if no hardware is available (never returns software). Default.
variants.atLeast(HardwareClass): Hard floor by class; fails unavailableTier below the class.
variants.bestEffort: Never fails on tier; may return tee/software with meetsFloor=false and honest evidence (the only variant that returns software).

TierPolicy.note: Selection is by class, never a concrete SecurityLevel; the achieved level is in SecurityTierReport.achieved.

HardwareClass.note: The atLeast(...) floor. discreteSecure = {secureEnclave, strongBox, tpm}; trustedEnvironment = {tee}. Matches security-level.json partialOrder classes.

AccessControlPolicy.authValiditySeconds: int? (null or 0 = per-use auth; > 0 = time window)

primitives.generateKey: (KeySpec) -> (KeyHandle, SecurityTierReport). Tier per KeySpec.tierPolicy (strongest/atLeast fail unavailableTier below floor; bestEffort never fails on tier); existing alias FAILS keyAlreadyExists.
primitives.sign: (KeyHandle, digest[32], SignOptions?) -> Signature. Async; host-UI biometric callback; a second concurrent auth-gated sign is rejected.
primitives.getAttestation: (KeyHandle) -> AttestationResult. No call-time challenge (bound at generateKey).
primitives.getSecurityTier: (KeyHandle) -> SecurityTierReport. On an invalidated key returns invalidated==true (no throw).
